novel_features.name: Invoice batch ingest
novel_features.description: Batch upload a folder of invoices to Zoho Expense, SHA256-dedup against the local store, poll autoscan in parallel, and auto-tag from learned merchant memory — the headline workflow for AI agents that ingest invoices from email.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps the upload-poll-tag-dedup loop that no competitor does because no competitor has a local store + learned merchant memory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents that ingest invoices on a cadence need one command that handles dedup, autoscan, and tagging — not three.

novel_features.name: Merchant memory
novel_features.description: Build a local merchant→category/project/tag mapping from sync history; pre-fill tags on first sight of a new merchant.
novel_features.rationale: Zoho only auto-categorizes a merchant after the org has seen + categorized it once. We bypass this by training locally and pre-populating tags via PUT /expenses/{id}.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Solves Zoho's 'first-time merchant is always uncategorized' limitation with zero API calls.

novel_features.name: Merchant mapping training
novel_features.description: Train the local merchant→category mapping for future auto-tag (one merchant at a time, or bulk from a CSV).
novel_features.rationale: The schema that powers `invoice ingest --auto-tag` lives in SQLite, not Zoho.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets the user (or agent) explicitly seed the auto-tag map without waiting for sync history to learn it.

novel_features.name: Receipt hash gate
novel_features.description: SHA256-hash incoming receipts at upload, refuse duplicates (use --force to override).
novel_features.rationale: Zoho has weak server-side dedup; uploading the same PDF twice creates two expenses. Our hash gate is the only way to prevent that without polling /expenses after every upload.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Idempotent receipt upload — safe to re-run on a folder agents have already processed.

novel_features.name: Monthly close
novel_features.description: Bundle a month's unreported expenses into an expense report in one shot — flags still-processing autoscans, flags untagged items, creates the report, attaches, optionally submits.
novel_features.rationale: Zoho's web UI takes 8 clicks to do this. Our local query plus generated endpoint calls collapses it into a single command, scriptable on cron.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The natural end of every monthly agent workflow — turn raw expenses into a submitted report without leaving the terminal.

novel_features.name: GST CGST/SGST split
novel_features.description: India-specific: parse an expense's tax_id and line items, compute CGST/SGST (intra-state) or IGST (inter-state) shares, emit the breakdown or update the expense.
novel_features.rationale: Zoho stores total tax but doesn't expose the GST component split. India tax filings need this breakdown — only possible with local enrichment from tax + line item data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns a monthly export into something a CA can directly file, without the user re-doing the math by hand.

novel_features.name: Untagged expense audit
novel_features.description: List expenses missing category_id or project_id; `--auto-fix` applies the merchant→tag memory map and PUTs back to Zoho.
novel_features.rationale: Closes the loop after autoscan — autoscanned expenses arrive with merchant_name set but typically without category/project. Our local merchant memory fills the gap in bulk.
novel_features.why_it_matters: One command turns 30 untagged scanned receipts into 30 tagged ones, using the user's own historical mapping.

narrative.headline: Upload receipts, auto-tag expenses from learned merchant memory, and close out the month in one command — the agent-friendly Zoho Expense CLI nobody else has built.
narrative.value_prop: Zoho ships autoscan; this CLI ships everything that comes after — receipt dedup, learned merchant→category mapping, India GST splitting, and monthly report bundling. Built for AI agents that ingest invoices from an inbox on a cadence and need an idempotent, structured-output upload path. India-region by default; works against any Zoho datacenter by overriding the base URL.
narrative.auth_narrative: Zoho Expense uses OAuth 2.0 with a self-client authorization-code flow. Create a self-client at https://api-console.zoho.in/, generate a 10-minute authorization code, then run `zoho-expense-pp-cli auth login --client-id=... --client-secret=... --code=...`. The CLI exchanges the code for a long-lived refresh token (stored at `~/.config/zoho-expense-pp-cli/config.toml`) and refreshes access tokens transparently. Tokens use the literal `Zoho-oauthtoken` Authorization prefix (not `Bearer`).

quickstart.comment: Verify auth, region, and API reachability before doing anything else

quickstart.command: zoho-expense-pp-cli org use $(zoho-expense-pp-cli organizations list --json | jq -r '.[0].organization_id')
quickstart.comment: Set the active organization (first one in the list)
Sync categories, reporting tags, projects, customers, expenses into the local store
Upload one receipt with hash-dedup and auto-tag
Batch ingest a folder for AI consumption
Bundle the month into a report and submit

troubleshoots.fix: Run `zoho-expense-pp-cli auth refresh` — the access token expired (1h lifetime). If that fails, the refresh token may have rotated out (max 20 per user); re-run `auth login` with a fresh authorization code.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 404 / endpoint not found from a .com token
troubleshoots.fix: Region mismatch. Your token was issued by accounts.zoho.com but you're targeting www.zohoapis.in. Override `base_url` in the config file or set ZOHO_EXPENSE_REGION accordingly.
File format or size issue — max 5MB; JPG/PNG/PDF only. Multi-page PDFs scan but slower. Try re-saving the PDF as image-only and retry.

troubleshoots.symptom: First expense for a new merchant has no category
troubleshoots.fix: Expected — Zoho only auto-categorizes after the merchant has been seen + categorized once. Use `merchant map '<name>' --category=...` to seed the local mapping, then `expense untagged --auto-fix`.
Zoho caps at 100 req/min per org. The CLI's adaptive limiter handles this automatically; if you see this surface, you're running parallel batches — drop concurrency.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an AI agent needs to ingest invoices from email (or any source) and post them to Zoho Expense on a recurring cadence. It's also the right tool for monthly close automation — bundling expenses into a report and submitting in one command — and for India-specific GST splitting that the Zoho web UI doesn't expose. Not the right tool for occasional one-off expense editing through the UI (Zoho's web app is fine for that).

recipes.title: Monthly Hermes flow
recipes.command: zoho-expense-pp-cli invoice ingest ~/Inbox/expenses --auto-tag --agent | tee /tmp/this-month.json && zoho-expense-pp-cli close --month=2026-05 --auto-submit
recipes.explanation: Ingest every saved invoice with hash dedup + auto-tagging, then bundle the month and submit. The pattern Hermes runs end-of-month. Use the current YYYY-MM in your own scripts.

recipes.title: Audit untagged expenses
recipes.explanation: Find untagged expenses over ₹1000 — the ones most worth manually tagging when auto-fix can't help.

recipes.title: Train the merchant memory
recipes.command: zoho-expense-pp-cli merchant list --agent --select merchant_name,category_name,seen_count | jq '.[] | select(.category_name == null)' | head -5
recipes.explanation: Find the top 5 merchants the local memory hasn't classified yet — feed them into `merchant map` to bootstrap the auto-tag map.

recipes.title: Search synced expenses
recipes.explanation: Full-text search across synced expenses — instant local query, no API call.

recipes.title: GST report for the CA
recipes.explanation: Compute a CGST/SGST/IGST breakdown for a single expense and emit a CSV row ready to hand to a chartered accountant. Loop over expense_ids in a shell pipeline to bulk-export a month.

trigger_phrases: close my zoho expenses for the month
